channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Apple announces $500 billion investment in US amid tariff threats that could affect the iPhone
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Apple announced Monday that it plans to invest more than $500 billion in the United States over the next four years, including plans to hire 20,000 people and build a new server factory in Texas.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Kyndryl: The largest manager of VMware assets is busier than ever in the cloud
item.description: With more than 80,000 employees and serving more than 75% of the Fortune 100 and customers in more than 60 countries, Kyndryl is uniquely qualified to speak to the rapidly evolving infrastructure needs of enterprises.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Apple Invests $500 Billion in US by 2029, Creating 20,000 New Jobs - Tech News
item.description: Apple's massive investment aims to support US manufacturing and R&D efforts, hiring 20,000 new workers over four years. Houston server facility and expanded Data Center capacity are part of the plan.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: The best apps to find new books
item.description: This article is republished with permission from Wonder Tools, a newsletter that helps you discover the most useful sites and apps. Subscribe here. Books offer a compelling, slower alternative to the onslaught of negative news.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Caplan, Fast Company

item.title: Black Ops 6 wasn't kidding, there really is something Tony Hawk's Pro Skater related happening on March 4
item.description: The Tony Hawk's Pro Skater website has been updated with a timer, lining up with that Black Ops 6 multiplayer easter egg.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: US intensifies scrutiny of the EU’s Digital Markets Act
item.description: In a significant escalation of transatlantic tensions over tech regulation, US House Judiciary Chairman Jim Jordan has demanded clarification from the EU antitrust chief Teresa Ribera regarding the enforcement of the EU’s digital marketplace rules, citing concerns about potential discrimination agai
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: 10 machine learning mistakes and how to avoid them
item.description: Machine learning technology is taking hold across many sectors as its applications are more widely adopted. The research firm Fortune Business Insights forecasts the global machine-learning market will expand from $26.03 billion in 2023 to $225.91 billion by 2030.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: 5 tips for mastering virtual communication
item.description: Andrew Brodsky is a management professor at McCombs School of Business at the University of Texas at Austin. He is also CEO of Ping Group and has received numerous awards, including being chosen by Poets & Quants as one of the Best 40-Under-40 MBA Professors of 2023.
item.dc:creator: Next Big Idea Club, Fast Company

item.title: Apple drops encryption feature for UK users after government reportedly demanded backdoor access
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Apple said Friday it will stop offering an advanced data security option for British users after the government reportedly demanded that the company provide backdoor access for any data those users have stored in the cloud.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A top Chinese official tours Thai-Myanmar border to highlight crackdown on scam centers
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — Efforts to shut down online scam centers in Myanmar appeared to gain momentum on Monday as a top Chinese security official visited both sides of the Thai-Myanmar border ahead of expected large-scale repatriations of workers in the illicit industry.
item.dc:creator: JINTAMAS SAKSORNCHAI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Neudesic launches in Australia in a bid to speed up AI adoption for IT teams
item.description: Artificial intelligence (AI) has gained significant traction among business leaders keen to explore ways it can drive operational efficiencies and cost savings. But while top leadership is sold on its potential, it’s a different tale for IT teams working the ground.
item.dc:creator: Network World
